title: Vertex Cloud
compiled_truth:
Vertex Cloud is one of the top-tier cloud infrastructure providers globally, holding roughly 18% of the enterprise market as of mid-2027. Headquartered in Portland, Oregon, Vertex operates 34 data center regions across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. The company has built its reputation on raw compute and storage reliability, though it's been increasingly aggressive about expanding its platform services layer — particularly around data pipeline tooling and managed networking.

The relationship between Vertex and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) became public in early 2027 when Vertex announced a native integration of Anchor's single-pass primitives into its managed data services tier. The deal was widely seen as a validation moment for Anchor's approach to deterministic data infrastructure, and it marked Vertex's first deep integration with a sub-Series-B startup in over two years. Internally, Vertex's platform engineering group had been evaluating Anchor's codec layer since late 2026 after benchmarks showed meaningful bandwidth and verification gains on cross-region transfers. The integration was championed by Vertex's VP of Platform, Dana Sorenson, who reportedly pushed for a tighter coupling than what shipped initially.

Vertex Cloud sits in an interesting competitive position. It doesn't have the sheer scale of the largest providers, but its enterprise penetration is disproportionately strong in regulated verticals — financial services, healthcare, and goverment. This is part of why Anchor's deterministic authentication properties were attractive: Vertex customers already care deeply about data integrity guarantees. The integration lets Vertex offer what it calls "verified transit" as a platform-native feature, which has become a differentiator in RFP processes.

There's been persistent speculation that Vertex may pursue an acquisition of [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec), especially after the integration deepened in Q3 2027. Vertex has the balance sheet for it — the company did $4.2B in revenue in its last fiscal year — and the strategic logic is straightforward. But Anchor's leadership has been publicly noncommital, and there are reasons to beleive they'd prefer to remain independent through at least 2028. Either way, Vertex is now Anchor's largest distribution channel by volume, and the relationship is one of the most consequential in Anchor's trajectory so far.
timeline:
- **2026-08-14** | Vertex Cloud platform engineering team begins internal evaluation of [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) primitives for cross-region data transfer optimization.
- **2026-11-02** | Dana Sorenson, VP of Platform at Vertex, visits Anchor's offices for a technical deep-dive with the core engineering team.
- **2027-01-19** | Vertex Cloud and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) publicly announce native integration of Anchor's single-pass primitives into Vertex's managed data services tier.
- **2027-03-07** | First Vertex customers gain access to "verified transit" feature in beta across three North American regions.
- **2027-05-22** | Vertex reports 12% reduction in cross-region transfer overhead on workloads using the Anchor integration during its quarterly infrastructure review.
- **2027-07-30** | Integration expanded to all 34 Vertex data center regions globally. Enterprise adoption ramps significantly in financial services vertical.
- **2027-09-11** | Industry analysts publish reports speculating on potential Vertex acquisition of Anchor Codec. Both companies decline to comment.
- **2027-11-15** | Vertex Cloud featured as a case study in Anchor's Series B fundraising materials.
- **2028-01-28** | Vertex announces a dedicated co-engineering team of 8 people focused on deepening Anchor integration across storage and networking layers.
- **2028-04-03** | Dana Sorenson gives keynote at Vertex's annual developer conference highlighting deterministic data infrastructure as a core platform direction.

_facts.name: Vertex Cloud
_facts.description: Major cloud provider with 18% enterprise market share; Anchor integration announced 2027
